title: Edge case IDs EC-S-005 and EC-S-010 are double-defined with conflicting meanings
description:
behavioral-spec.md defines EC-S-005 and EC-S-010 twice each, with mutually inconsistent semantics. Inside REQ-FILTER-003 (lines 204-206) the spec states:
  - EC-S-005: 'Esc on already-empty input: SearchCleared may be dispatched but has no visible effect'
  - EC-S-010: 'Esc while debounce timer is pending: timer is cancelled BEFORE SearchCleared is dispatched'

In the §4.1 Edge Case Catalog table (lines 449-455) the same IDs are reassigned:
  - EC-S-005: 'Rapid keystroke followed by Esc before debounce'
  - EC-S-010: 'Esc key with no pending debounce'

The two scenarios behind EC-S-005 are roughly inverse (already-empty input vs. typed-then-Esc-mid-debounce); the two scenarios behind EC-S-010 are also inverse (Esc with pending timer vs. Esc with no pending timer). Tests that cite EC-S-005/EC-S-010 by ID (e.g. PROP-FILTER-024 references EC-S-005 and EC-S-010) cannot determine which behavior they must verify. This is a hard internal contradiction that blocks the EARS → test traceability that Done items 7-8 rely on.

evidence.snippet:
REQ-FILTER-003 inline:
- EC-S-005: Esc on already-empty input ...
- EC-S-010: Esc while debounce timer is pending ...

§4.1 catalog row 5: EC-S-005 | Rapid keystroke followed by Esc before debounce ...
§4.1 catalog row 10: EC-S-010 | Esc key with no pending debounce ...

remediation: Renumber so each EC-S-NNN ID has exactly one definition. Suggested split: keep EC-S-005 = 'rapid keystroke + Esc before debounce' (table) and assign new IDs (e.g. EC-S-016, EC-S-017) to 'Esc on already-empty input' and 'Esc with pending timer'. Update PROP-FILTER-024 and any test references accordingly.
